rights.title: Right to know grounds of arrest
rights.description: You have the right to be informed of the grounds (reasons) for your arrest within 24 hours.

rights.title: Right to consult lawyer
rights.description: You have the absolute right to consult a lawyer of your choice. This is a constitutional right that cannot be denied.

rights.title: Right against self-incrimination
rights.description: You cannot be forced to confess or give evidence against yourself. Confessions made under coercion are invalid.

rights.title: Right to bail
rights.description: For bailable offences, bail is your right, not a privilege. For non-bailable offences, bail is discretionary but should be granted unless flight risk or tampering is likely.

rights.title: Right to speedy trial
rights.description: You have the constitutional right to a speedy trial. Unreasonable delay in chargesheet or trial is grounds for bail.

rights.title: Section 436A - Mandatory Bail
rights.description: After being detained for half of the maximum sentence, you MUST be released on bail. This is not discretionary.

rights.title: Default Bail - Section 167
rights.description: If chargesheet is not filed within 60 days (normal) or 90 days (serious crimes), you are eligible for bail automatically.

rights.title: Right to medical examination
rights.description: You have the right to medical examination if you suspect police torture or abuse. Medical report can be used as evidence.

rights.title: Right to free legal aid
rights.description: If you cannot afford a lawyer, the state will provide you with one free of cost. You can also approach the legal aid board.

rights.title: Right to challenge custody
rights.description: You can file a habeas corpus petition to challenge illegal detention or apply for bail in higher courts if lower court denies it.
